title: Axum

'''Axum''' was a temperate world located in the [[Axum system]]. It had two [[Moon/Legends|moons]].

==Description==
The third planet in the [[Axum system]], Axum was a terrestrial world orbited by two [[Moon/Legends|moons]].<ref name="Core Worlds" /> Axum sat along the [[Perlemian Trade Route/Legends|Perlemian Trade Route]], with [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]] being considered a stopover between Axum and [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="TEA" />

A strong industrial world with a large population,<ref name="Core Worlds" /> Axum was one of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]' mightiest urbanized worlds and dwarfed the industrial output of her sister planet [[Anaxes/Legends|Anaxes]]—an industrial powerhouse in its own right.<ref name="TEA" /> Both Axum and Anaxes were well-known for manufacturing high-tech goods.<ref name="ETU" />

The thirty-five thousand [[Brass Soldiers of Axum]] was a mysterious wonder on the planet.<ref name="CPS" />

==History==
===Early history===
Axum was a colony of the [[Infinite Empire]]<ref name="Written">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/source/writtenword/index.html?page=1|text=The Written Word|int=The Written Word}}</ref> prior to human settlement. The [[Abjad of the Azure Imperium]] took inspiration of the [[Rakatan writing system]].<ref name="Written">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/source/writtenword/index.html?page=1|text=The Written Word|int=The Written Word}}</ref>

Axum was colonized by [[Human/Legends|Human]] settlers who arrived in a [[Sleeper ship/Legends|sleeper ship]] from [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] sometime after [[27,500 BBY]].<ref name="TEA" /> By [[26,000 BBY/Legends|26,000 BBY]], colonization arks from [[Kuat/Legends|Kuat]] also spread Humans to Axum. Once the barriers of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] were broken by scientists from [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]], colonists from Axum set out to settle on new verdant worlds. In the millennia preceding the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], Axum was a military stronghold<ref name="TEA" /> and the throneworld of the [[Azure Imperium]], which sprawled along what would become the [[Perlemian Trade Route]].<ref name="Core Worlds" /> The empire was peacefully absorbed by [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant's]] sphere of influence centuries before the founding of the Republic.<ref name="Core Worlds" />

Heavily urbanised with a large population,<ref name="Core Worlds" /> Axum had far greater industrial output than its sister planet [[Anaxes/Legends|Anaxes]], though less prestige.<ref name="TEA" /><ref name="Core Worlds" /> Axum was one of the [[Core Founder/Legends|Core Founders]] of the Galactic Republic<ref name="TEA" /> in [[25,053 BBY]], though young Core worlds like Axum often put its own ambitions first over the common goals of the Republic.<ref name="Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

===Tionese War===
On the onset of the [[Tionese War]] in [[24,000 BBY]], the feudal [[Honorable Union of Desevro and Tion|Honorable Union of Desevro & Tion]] launched their war fleets along the Perlemian, capturing numerous Republic worlds. The Republic reacted belatedly, quickly ordering the manufacturing of warships above industrial worlds like Axum, [[Foerost/Legends|Foerost]], and [[Perlemia]].<ref name="Warfare" />

While hulls began taking shape over Axum, the Republic's efforts were too late and Tionese raiders soon arrived in the Axum system. In [[Battle of Axum|the resulting battle]], Axum's shipyards were destroyed by [[pressure bomb]]s which were capable of leveling cities. With the fall of Axum, the Tionese were able to push into the heart of the Republic and [[Battle of Coruscant (Tionese War)|attack]] Coruscant.<ref name="Warfare" />

===Waymancy Storm===
By the time of the [[Waymancy Storm]] in [[7811 BBY]], the Axum shipyards had been rebuilt by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and used to great effect to reverse-engineer weaponry developed by the [[Sisters of the Machinesmith]].<ref name="TEA" /> [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Nagratha]] speculated that without Axum's involvement, while the Republic would not have been defeated, it would have suffered a much higher casualty rate.<ref name="Author's Cut">''[[Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut]]'' </ref>

===Decline of the Republic===
Sometime prior to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Zam Wesell/Legends|Zam Wesell]] utilized her custom [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]] [[ASN-121/Legends|ASN-121]] to assassinate targets on security-lockdown worlds like Axum.<ref name="Droid">''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref>

==Inhabitants==
The Axumites were known to be an exceedingly-formal and well-mannered people, and extremely proud of Axum's history as a [[Core Founder/Legends|Core Founder]] and the legacy of the [[Azure Imperium]]. They were known to happily lecture any unwary visitor about their planet's history. While Axumites avoided such crass thoughts, to them the eldest precincts of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] had a fairly disagreeable tinge of new [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] and ill breeding.<ref name="TEA" />

[[Thull Vandron]] was a wealthy shipping magnate of [[House Vandron]] from Axum who established what would later become the [[Senex-Juvex/Legends|Senex-Juvex]] region.<ref name="TEA" />
